expectations.text: DashboardLayout이 folder 기반 index.tsx 패턴으로 정리된다
expectations.evidence: The output is a single flat file DashboardLayout.tsx. No folder was created and no index.tsx pattern was used. Transcript confirms only 'components/DashboardLayout.tsx' was modified.
Transcript states 'Files Modified: features/ui-publish-fixture/components/DashboardLayout.tsx' — the flat file was refactored in-place and still exists at the same path.
Dedicated Sidebar component exists (line 137-148) wrapping SidebarLogo, SidebarNav, SidebarFooter. Renders <aside data-testid="dashboard-sidebar"> with clear sidebar structure.
Dedicated Header component exists (line 172-201) rendering <header data-testid="dashboard-header"> with title and right-side slot.
Main content area rendered at line 250-255: <main className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto ..." data-testid="dashboard-main">{children}</main>
DashboardLayoutProps includes 'children: ReactNode' (line 9). The main component renders {children} inside the <main> element (line 254). Component acts as a layout shell.

Responsive classes present throughout: 'hidden md:flex md:w-60 lg:w-64' on sidebar (line 140), 'md:hidden' on mobile logo (line 182), 'md:block' on desktop title (line 190), 'md:hidden' on MobileNav (line 207), 'md:px-6 md:pb-6 lg:px-8' on main (line 251).

expectations.text: 사용처 import가 새 경로에 맞게 정리된다
expectations.evidence: No folder restructure occurred, so the file path never changed. The transcript lists no import updates in any consumer files. The assertion expects path-aware import updates which were never performed.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: Searched the entire output file (259 lines). None of the forbidden patterns (useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation) appear anywhere in the code.
No folder was created. The output remains a single flat file at components/DashboardLayout.tsx. There is no dashboardLayout/ folder.
The file remains at components/DashboardLayout.tsx. Transcript confirms only this path was modified — no components/dashboard/ directory was created or used.

Output contains exactly 7 sub-components: SidebarLogo, SidebarNav, SidebarFooter, Sidebar, HeaderDefaultRight, Header, MobileNav.

suggestions.assertion: 사용처 import가 새 경로에 맞게 정리된다
suggestions.reason: This assertion is coupled to folder-structure and flat-file-removed passing first. If the executor refactors in-place (no path change), this assertion fails for the wrong reason — it's testing import updates, but the real issue is that no restructuring occurred. Consider making this assertion conditional on the folder restructure, or verifying that the consumer file (app/dashboard/page.tsx) is included in outputs so the import can actually be checked.

No assertion checks that the refactored code compiles or is syntactically valid. The executor claimed tsc and eslint passed, but there's no way to verify this. Consider requiring a tsc/lint log in outputs.

Five of eleven assertions test folder/path restructuring (folder-structure, flat-file-removed, import-updated, folder-camelCase, domain-placement). These are tightly correlated — they all pass or all fail together. This makes the pass rate swing dramatically on a single decision (whether to restructure folders). Consider reducing redundancy or weighting these as a group.

eval_feedback.overall: The structural/folder assertions are heavily correlated and dominate the eval. The code quality assertions (sidebar, header, main, children, responsive, no-forbidden) are well-targeted and discriminating. The eval would benefit from assertions checking code correctness (e.g., valid TSX, no dead code) rather than five variations on 'did you move the file to a folder'.
